articles.title: RUN THE PROBLEM, NOT THE UPDATE
articles.summary: Builder PMs run problems, not updates, by engaging leadership around a clear question and decisions. They drive convergence by separating messy exploration from focused executive narratives.

articles.title: COMMERCIAL VS INTERNAL PRODUCTS
articles.summary: Commercial products must win in the market, not just solve problems, making them far harder than internal tools. Success depends on strong product discovery to outperform alternatives and drive real outcomes.

articles.title: BETTING ON HUMANS WITH EXPERTISE
articles.summary: The New York Times builds a daily habit by bundling journalism with lifestyle products and maintaining direct user relationships. In an AI world, it wins through human expertise, trusted brand, and destination experiences.

articles.title: FROM SIGNUP TO VALUE: HOW AI IS CHANGING ACTIVATION IN SAAS
articles.summary: AI enables instant value but weakens retention when users stay passive. The best products turn early value into habits through action, return triggers, and accumulated context.

articles.title: THE GOLDEN RULES OF AGENT-FIRST PRODUCT ENGINEERING
articles.summary: Agent-first products treat AI agents as a core interface, giving them full capabilities, clean abstractions, and the right context to act independently. Strong systems combine agent autonomy with human judgment through thoughtful design, skills, and continuous iteration.

articles.title: HOW TO BUILD A TEAM OS IN CLAUDE CODE WITH HANNAH STULBERG (70 MINUTE PODCAST)
articles.summary: AI-native teams use a shared “Team OS” repository so agents can access structured context and work autonomously. Success depends on clean context, deliberate planning, and continuous team contribution.

articles.title: DEMAND MIX, DISCOVERY, AND AI AS A (DYS)FUNCTION MULTIPLIER
articles.summary: Teams are shaped by their demand mix and how they manage it, not just their processes. AI amplifies these dynamics, accelerating either chaos or learning depending on how demand is shaped.

articles.title: WE'RE ALL DOING THE SAME JOB NOW
articles.summary: AI is collapsing PM, design, and engineering into one blended role. What remains are durable advantages: judgment, systems thinking, and organizational power.

articles.title: BYOAI
articles.summary: AI may become “bring your own AI,” as users carry their preferred tools from home to work.

articles.title: AI STARTUP ROCKET OFFERS VIBE MCKINSEY-STYLE REPORTS AT A FRACTION OF THE COST
articles.summary: Rocket helps users decide what to build by generating consulting-style product strategies in a world where coding is easy.
